title: Check Traffic Before Leaving
summary: Look up current traffic conditions to decide when to leave or which route to take.
learnerPromise: You will be able to check traffic conditions on a map app and use that information to choose your departure time or route.
whyItMatters: Checking traffic before you leave helps you avoid unexpected delays, reduce stress, and arrive closer to your planned time. It also helps you pick a faster or smoother route.
realLifeUses: Checking if your school commute has heavy traffic so you can leave earlier
Comparing two routes to a doctor's appointment to pick the faster one
Seeing if an accident is blocking your usual route and choosing an alternative
Planning when to leave for an event to avoid rush hour delays
materialsNeeded: A smartphone or computer with a map app
result: You know the current traffic level and estimated travel time for your route.
youWillLearn: How to read traffic color codes on a map (green, yellow, red)
How to compare estimated travel times for different routes
How to spot traffic incidents or road closures on the map
How to decide whether to leave earlier or take a different route
miniChallenge: Open a map app and check the traffic for a route you take regularly. Compare the usual route with one alternative route. Note the estimated time for each and any traffic delays shown.
steps: Open your map app and enter your destination.
Turn on the traffic layer if it is not already visible.
Look at the color-coded roads along your route to spot delays.
Compare your usual route with any alternative routes the app suggests.
Note the estimated arrival time and decide if you should leave earlier or take a different route.
completionCriteria: You viewed the traffic conditions for at least one route
You compared estimated times between two route options
You made a decision about when to leave or which route to take
commonProblems: Traffic data can be outdated or change suddenly after you check
Alternative routes may look shorter but take longer due to local roads
Apps sometimes suggest routes that involve difficult turns or tolls
tips: Check traffic five to ten minutes before you leave in case conditions change
Consider leaving earlier even if traffic looks light, just in case
Familiar routes with lighter traffic are often more reliable than unfamiliar shortcuts
